description: This figure presents experimental and simulated microstructure evolutions in low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) during hot compression at 950°C with a transient strain rate decrease from 0.1 s⁻¹ to 0.01 s⁻¹ at 0.4 strain. Part (a) shows EBSD IPF (inverse pole figure) maps (upper row) and GOS (grain orientation spread) maps (lower row) at three strains: 0.4 (left), 0.5 (center), and 0.9 (right). The IPF maps reveal grain orientation distributions with average grain sizes of 81.50±6.32 μm, 52.92±3.35 μm, and 50.71±3.29 μm, respectively. The GOS maps quantify dynamic recrystallization (DRX) fractions, showing a dramatic increase from 38.9% at 0.4 strain to 95.2% at 0.5 strain and 97.7% at 0.9 strain, indicating rapid completion of recrystallization following the strain rate reduction. Part (b) displays corresponding 3D cellular automaton simulation results, where white represents the unrecrystallized matrix, green denotes grains formed during the first stage (0.1 s⁻¹), and red indicates grains nucleated during the second stage (0.01 s⁻¹). The simulated DRX fractions evolve from 42.9% at 0.4 strain to 99.1% at 0.5 strain and 99.8% at 0.9 strain, with average grain sizes of 82.68 μm, 50.78 μm, and 52.58 μm, respectively, validating the model's capability to capture multi-stage DRX kinetics under varying deformation conditions.
